name: Family Casserole
recipeIngredient: -5 medium to large Russet potatoes, peeled
-1 lb Firm tofu, wrapped or frozen and defrosted
-1 tbsp Instant gravy mix or other strong powdered broth
-1 cup Chopped spinach or green chard
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and quarter the potatoes. Place them in a medium or large pot with water to cover. Add garlic, celery, parsley, peppercorns, onion, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer over medium-low heat for 15 to 20 minutes, or until very tender.
2. While the potatoes are cooking, prepare the filling. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Sauté onion and garlic for 1 minute over medium heat, then add mushrooms and sauté for 2 minutes. Crumble tofu into the skillet and briefly sauté, mixing well. Stir in barbecue sauce, nutritional yeast (optional), gravy mix or broth cube, thyme, paprika, and tamari. Mix well and sauté, stirring frequently, for 20 minutes over medium heat. Preheat oven to 400°F.
3. Transfer potatoes from the water to a large bowl, reserving potato stock. Add miso, olive oil, and 3/4 to 1 cup of potato stock to the potatoes in ¼ to ⅓-cup increments, mashing as you add the stock. Add only enough water to moisten the potatoes adequately. Do not overmoisten.
4. Add corn and spinach to the filling and mix well. Pour the filling into an oiled, shallow ovenproof casserole dish. Pat down with the back of a large spoon. Spread potato crust evenly over the filling, smoothing the top with a spoon or spatula. Dust evenly with paprika. Bake for 30 to 40 minutes, or until the crust is golden.
5. While the casserole bakes, prepare the gravy. Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Add flour and nutritional yeast (if using) and stir with a whisk over medium heat to form a paste. Slowly stir in 2 ½ cups of reserved potato water, whisking as you stir to allow the gravy to thicken. Stir in instant gravy mix and continue whisking until the gravy is thick and smooth; add additional potato water if necessary.
6. Serve casserole with crust on the bottom and filling on top. Spoon gravy over the top.
